$comment: Single source of truth for the .rvsc container/record format, the EPROM_* setting identifier table, known flag bits, and enum labels. Both rvsc.py (loaded at runtime) and docs/index.html (injected at build time by tools/build_web.py) derive every name/flag/enum from this file. Do not duplicate any of these literal tables elsewhere.

mk2_info.name: Mk2vscInfo
mk2_info.$comment: 10 bytes exactly (nameLen=10), name 'Mk2vscInfo' with NO trailing '='. An earlier draft of FORMAT.md listed the name as 'Mk2vscInfo=' because the low byte of the following u32 absolute_end_offset happened to be a printable ASCII character in the reference file examined; a parser must read exactly nameLen bytes for the name (as this tool's parse_sections() does), never infer the name boundary by scanning for printable runs. See FORMAT.md 'Naming artifact' note.

record_layout.$comment: BareSettingInfo is a packed array of fixed-size records, one per setting index, no padding. BareSettingData is a parallel packed array of raw u16 values, same index order.

decode.$comment: real = (raw + offset) / abs(scale) when scale < 0; real = (raw + offset) * scale when scale > 0; scale == 0 means the record has no linear numeric meaning (bitfield/reserved slot) and decodes to null. min/max/default must be run through this SAME transform before being compared to a decoded value - comparing a decoded value against undecoded raw min/max is a unit mismatch and was a real bug found during development.

alignment.$comment: Neither array's start offset is fixed across files/firmware. The correct start is found by brute-force self-validation: try candidate offsets for both arrays and score each candidate by how well its decoded values fall within their own declared [min, max]. See ALGORITHM.md-equivalent notes in rvsc.py/build_web.py for why RANGE-WEIGHTED scoring (not raw match count, and not a fixed tie-break tolerance window) was chosen.
alignment.confidence: high (technique); per-file result must independently satisfy min_valid_record_fraction

naming.$comment: Setting index i (i >= 1) maps to epron_names[i - 1]. Index 0 has no name in every file examined (it is an unused/sentinel record, verified by scale == 0). If a file has more records than epron_names entries, extras fall back to 'setting_<n>' - names are never invented.

flags.$comment: Each entry names the setting (by EPROM_* name, resolved through 'naming' above at load time - not by a fixed absolute file offset, since that offset moves with alignment) plus a bit position and a human label. LOWER (medium) confidence than the numeric settings table: bit meanings were resolved via VEConfigure's own UI form definitions, not re-derived from each file's own self-describing min/max the way numeric settings are. Extended additively: the original 4 entries (EBIT_DisableCharge, EBIT_EnableReducedFloat, EBIT_WeakACInput, EBIT_LithiumBattery) keep their original id/setting_name/bit/label values unchanged; all 43 entries (4 original + 39 newly added) gained rtti_ordinal/basis/certainty/ui_tab/ui_group/ui_label/notes/inverted fields from the RTTI-validated mapping in rvsc-mapping.json (see 'word_order'/'bit_formula' below). 'label' on the 39 new entries is the VEConfigure UI text (ui_label) where confirmed, else a mechanical CamelCase-split of the identifier (NOT a confirmed VEConfigure UI label) - check 'certainty' before displaying 'label' as authoritative.

flags.bit_formula: VEConfig.exe's Delphi RTTI recovers all 80 EBIT_*/pEBIT_* names as one contiguous ordinal run 0..79 (64 EBIT_ names, ordinals 0-63, then 16 pEBIT_ names, ordinals 64-79). word_index = ordinal // 16 (0..4, indexing into word_order above); bit = ordinal % 16. Validated 4/4 against every flag bit for which independent (differential or pre-existing) evidence existed; no case contradicted it. Both implementations should derive a flag's (word, bit) from 'rtti_ordinal' via this formula rather than hardcoding bit numbers per flag.
flags.certainty_note: The blanket 'confidence' field above predates RTTI validation; each item's own 'certainty' (confirmed/probable/unknown) and 'basis' now supersede it per-entry. 23 of 43 bits are 'confirmed', 7 'probable', 13 'unknown' (named via RTTI but with no UI or differential evidence yet).

enums.$comment: Raw integer value -> human label, keyed by EPROM_* setting name. Confidence matches the numeric settings table (high) since the enum values themselves are the same self-describing raw field, but the specific label wording is cross-referenced against VEConfigure's own UI, so it is reported at medium confidence like flags. EPROM_vsUsage and EPROM_GridCode added from rvsc-ui-spec.json ('Specify virtual switch usage' / 'Country / grid code standard' fields), ordinal-indexed (0-based) exactly as VEConfigure lists them; see 'certainty' above for per-identifier confidence. Battery type (10 options) and AES type (2 options) are NOT included here: no EPROM_* identifier is confirmed or even probable as their backing scalar (only the lithium/lead-acid boolean component of Battery type is known - EBIT_LithiumBattery). Their option text is preserved as UI layout only under 'ui_layout' / 'ui_field_coverage' below (certainty 'unmapped'), not as a decode-time enum, so nothing here falsely implies a confirmed raw-value mapping.

certainty.$comment: Per-identifier certainty for the enums above, from rvsc-mapping-report.md Method 1/2/3. EPROM_ChargeCharacteristic and EPROM_vsUsage are direct identifier/signature matches (confirmed); EPROM_GridCode's option TEXT is a verbatim, exhaustively observed VEConfigure list (23 entries, high confidence as a UI layout artifact) but which raw ordinal index EPROM_GridCode uses for which entry is only an identifier-name match, not yet differentially confirmed (probable).

record_types.$comment: Marks records whose raw u16 is a BITFIELD (a container of independent boolean/enum sub-fields), not a scalar quantity. A bitfield must NEVER be range-checked against its declared numeric [min, max] (that check is only meaningful for a scalar) - see rvsc-outofrange.md for the root-cause investigation: EPROM_FlagsWord0's bit 15 pushed its raw value past a numeric max that VEConfigure recorded for reasons unrelated to the word's actual legitimate bit range, dragging the alignment self-check to 143/144 in both reference files. Both rvsc.py's find_alignment/build_settings and docs/index.html's findAlignment/parseRvsc must read THIS list (not hardcode identifier names) when deciding whether to run the [min, max] check for a given setting, so the exclusion rule lives in exactly one place for both implementations.
record_types.bitfield_names_basis: EPROM_FlagsWord0/1/2/3 and EPROM_PermanentFlags0 are the five 16-bit words whose individual bits are named EBIT_*/pEBIT_* in VEConfig.exe's own Delphi RTTI (see 'flags' below) - i.e. every bit position in these five words has a real, independently-recovered identifier, which is structural proof the whole word is a bitfield container rather than a scalar. EPROM_FlagsWord3 currently has zero individually-named/confirmed bits (see flags.word_order note) but is included on the same evidence as the other three FlagsWord* words: identical naming pattern, identical 10-byte info-record shape, and it is already degenerate (min_raw == max_raw == 0) in the one reference file examined, which independently confirms a numeric [min,max] check is meaningless for it.

decode_notes.$comment: Per-identifier notes on decode behaviour that could otherwise look like a bug and get 'fixed' twice, independently, in the two implementations. These are informational only: decode_value()'s existing '(raw + offset) / abs(scale)' rule already produces the correct signed real value for every field below without any special-casing or reinterpretation of the raw u16 as signed - see rvsc-outofrange.md finding (b), which directly tested reinterpreting a bitfield's raw value as i16 and found it does NOT apply here (that was ruled out as the explanation for the FlagsWord0 anomaly).

items.note: Genuinely decodes to a negative real value (~-32.4 mV/degC class value observed in a reference file: raw=415 decodes to +0.0324 when offset=0, i.e. the SIGN comes from how the UI presents the compensation slope, not from a negative raw value). scale < 0 already means divide, so this is the ordinary negative-scale path used by many fields, not a distinct 'signed' code path.

settings_meta.$comment: Per-setting-index VEConfigure UI mapping (which tab/group/label/unit a decoded setting corresponds to), merged from rvsc-mapping.json - itself built from three independent methods (see 'basis_legend'): differential file-diffing against a known ground-truth change list, unique-decoded-signature matching, and identifier-name matching against VEConfig.exe's own Delphi RTTI symbol table (which independently validated 'naming.epron_names' above with ZERO mismatches over all 192 entries - see 'rtti_validation'). Keyed by setting index (string, matches naming: index i>=1 -> epron_names[i-1]). 'certainty' is confirmed/probable/unknown; 'unknown' certainty with basis 'unmapped' means no VEConfigure UI correspondence is asserted at all - identifier/index data is still valid, only the UI label is missing. Pinning a currently-unmapped or probable index later (e.g. via a targeted VEConfigure round-trip, see 'known_gaps' below) is a pure edit of one entry here - no code change in either implementation is needed to pick it up.
settings_meta.rtti_validation: The RTTI parse recovered 64 EBIT_ names + 16 pEBIT_ names + 192 EPROM_ names in one contiguous, correctly-ordered run of Delphi Pascal short-strings. The 192 EPROM_ names matched core/settings.json's epron_names table with ZERO mismatches, fully validating that existing table. Flag word/bit assignments (ordinal//16, ordinal%16) were cross-validated 4/4 against all previously-known or differential-confirmed flag bits.

basis_legend.differential: Direct empirical evidence: the two reference files differ in exactly these settings, and the direction/magnitude of each observed change matches a known, task-specified VEConfigure UI change. Strongest basis.
basis_legend.signature-unique: The setting's decoded (default,min,max) signature is unique across all indices in the file AND matches a documented VEConfigure UI default/range (task ground truth) or is otherwise self-consistent and unambiguous.
basis_legend.identifier: The EPROM_*/EBIT_* identifier name (recovered from VEConfig.exe's Delphi RTTI) self-evidently names the UI field, or follows a clearly-established naming convention (vson/vston/vsoff/vstoff) shared with other already-confirmed fields in the same group.
basis_legend.unmapped: No mapping asserted; reason given in 'notes'.

confidence_summary.note: ui_fields counts are over the 102 VEConfigure UI fields in 'ui_layout' (see rvsc-mapping-report.md Summary): 46 confirmed, 46 probable, 10 unmapped. This is a field-level count, distinct from the per-setting-index 'items' below (200 raw indices, most of which have no UI field at all - internal/reserved slots).

ui_layout.$comment: Verbatim VEConfigure 3 tab/group/field presentation layout, source rvsc-ui-spec.json (observed against VEConfigure 1.33, device MultiPlus 24/1200/25-16, firmware 2667558 - see 'tested_against' at the top of this file). This is the ONE place either implementation should read tab names, group names, field labels/types/units/enum option text, and mode-dependent visibility notes from - do not duplicate this literal layout elsewhere. field_no is the flattened 1..102 numbering used to join a field to its setting-index/flag-bit mapping certainty via 'certainty'/'mapped_via' (sourced from 'ui_field_coverage' in rvsc-mapping.json). A field with certainty 'unmapped' has no confirmed backing index/bit at all - its label/type/unit/options are still accurate VEConfigure UI text, they just cannot yet be filled in from decoded .rvsc data. Fields whose 'notes' mention mode-dependent visibility (e.g. 'Shown in lead-acid mode', 'Visible when Enable battery monitor is on') are gating rules implied by the VEConfigure UI itself, carried over verbatim - neither implementation should re-derive or hardcode them separately. DISPLAY CONVERSION (added after a VEConfigure cross-check found several time/rate fields decoding correctly but LABELLED with the wrong unit): 'unit' is always the DISPLAY unit (what VEConfigure's own UI shows). Where the info-table's own scale/offset decode a value into a DIFFERENT unit than 'unit' claims, the field also carries 'stored_unit' (documentation of what the decoded/raw-scale value 's.value'/'v.value' is actually in) plus 'display_divisor' and 'display_decimals': the number actually printed is (decoded_value / display_divisor) formatted to display_decimals places, and BOTH rvsc.py and docs/index.html must compute it this way from these two fields rather than re-deriving or hardcoding the arithmetic. A negative display_divisor both rescales AND flips sign in one step (see field 46's notes for why that's needed, not just a magnitude fix). Fields with no 'display_divisor' are unchanged from the decoded value (already in its display unit). A field may also carry 'display_note': a short caveat string that both implementations must render inline next to the value (not just as a tooltip) because the value's real-world meaning cannot be fully confirmed from decoded data alone (e.g. an enable/disable companion checkbox with no known backing bit) - see field 48.

fields.unit_notes: Decoded value (info-table scale=15 on the reference file) is in MINUTES, not hours: default raw=4 decodes to 60, and 60min = 1hr matches the documented Victron 24V default 'repeated absorption 1 hour' and VEConfigure's own displayed '1.00 Hr' for this same file. display_divisor=60 converts minutes to the displayed hours; display_decimals=2 matches VEConfigure's own 2-decimal-place display for this specific field (unlike fields 44/45, which VEConfigure displays as whole hours).

fields.unit_notes: Decoded value (info-table scale=360 on the reference file) is in MINUTES, not days: default raw=28 decodes to 10080, and 10080min = 7 days (7*24*60) exactly, matching the documented Victron 24V default 'repeated absorption interval 7 days' and VEConfigure's own displayed '7.00 Days' for this same file. display_divisor=1440 (24*60) converts minutes to displayed days.

fields.unit_notes: Decoded value (info-table scale=60) is in MINUTES: default raw=8 decodes to 480, and 480min = 8hr matches the documented Victron 24V default 'maximum absorption 8 hours' and VEConfigure's own displayed '8 Hr' (whole hours, no decimals) for the lead-acid reference file. display_divisor=60 converts minutes to displayed hours.

fields.label: Absorption time
fields.notes: Shown in lithium mode; replaces Maximum absorption time.
fields.mapped_via: idx10 EPROM_AbsorptionHours (differential-confirmed, lithium label -- same index as field 44)
fields.unit_notes: Same underlying minutes-valued setting as field 44 (idx10): on the lithium reference file raw=1 decodes to 60min = 1hr, matching VEConfigure's own displayed '1 Hr' (whole hours, no decimals) for this file. display_divisor=60 converts minutes to displayed hours.

fields.label: Temperature compensation
fields.notes: Lead-acid mode only.
fields.unit_notes: Decoded value (info-table scale=-12800) is an UNSIGNED magnitude in V/degC, not mV/degC: default raw=415 decodes to 0.032421875, matching the documented Victron default '0.0324 V/degC' exactly. The info-table's own min/max for this record (0 and 0.06 V/degC) prove the RAW/decoded representation can never be negative in this format -- there is no sign bit or negative-scale trick available to produce a signed value here, and the raw value was observed IDENTICAL (415) between a lead-acid file and a lithium file, ruling out a per-file sign flag too. Yet VEConfigure itself displays this same file's value as '-32.4 mV/degC' (confirmed by direct observation of the running application, and independently corroborated by CASE-STUDY.md's blind prediction/confirmation of '-32.4 mV/degC' against a second, unrelated device). Conclusion: the negative sign is a FIXED, vendor-side display convention baked into the UI -- not a property of the stored data -- because temperature compensation always SUBTRACTS from the target charge voltage as battery temperature rises (see CASE-STUDY.md's Root Cause section, where the compensation is described as 'further lowering' the effective target). No flag/bit was found anywhere in this table's 'flags' section that could plausibly carry a sign toggle for this setting (checked EBIT_* identifiers for Temp/Comp/Sign matches: none). display_divisor=-0.001 folds both the x1000 unit conversion (V -> mV) and this fixed sign convention into one number so neither implementation has to special-case the sign separately from the unit conversion. Certainty of the MAGNITUDE match is confirmed (exact numeric match against a documented default); certainty of the SIGN convention rests on the two independent VEConfigure observations above rather than on anything decodable from the file itself, so treat the sign specifically as evidence-based-but-not-file-verifiable if a future file family is found where it does not hold.

fields.display_note: Enable-state not decodable: this value is shown whenever this file is in lithium mode, but field 49 ('Enable stop charger below') has no confirmed backing flag anywhere in this table, so this decoder cannot tell whether the threshold is actually active. VEConfigure was observed displaying this same stored value (-20.50 degC) while its own 'Enable stop charger below' checkbox was UNCHECKED -- i.e. the vendor tool itself shows the stored value unconditionally, independent of the checkbox. Both implementations must therefore keep showing the stored value (hiding or greying it would assert an inactive state we cannot confirm from the file, which would be a guess in the other direction), but must surface this caveat inline rather than presenting the value as unconditionally in-effect.

unresolved_discrepancies.detail: Ground truth lists 'temperature compensation -32.4 -> removed/zeroed' as one of 17 changes between two reference files. EPROM_TempCompensationSlope (idx88, signature-confirmed, default 0.0324 exactly matching ground truth) did NOT change (raw 415 in both files). Probable explanation: the field is hidden by the UI when the lithium flag is on, rather than zeroed on disk - but this is UNCONFIRMED. Indices 193-196 (beyond the named 192-entry identifier table) did change between the files, but none decode to anything resembling -32.4 under any tested scale and all report scale=0 in this file, so this claim could not be positively pinned to an index.

unresolved_discrepancies.topic: idx66 / idx75 share an identical decoded signature
unresolved_discrepancies.detail: EPROM_ChargePercentageResetValueForBatteryMonitor (idx66) and EPROM_AbsToFloatSOCReset (idx75) both decode to (default=85, min=30, max=100) - signature matching alone cannot distinguish them. Only idx66 changed (85->95) between the two reference files, matching ground truth's LiFePO4-preset value of 95% exactly; idx75 stayed at 100 in both. A caution against trusting signature-only matching without a second, independent method.

Only partially placed: idx55/56/59/60 tentatively assigned to the 'VS options' Udc quartet (UI fields 96-99). RTTI names EBIT_vs2Invert and EBIT_vs2offWhenAC1Available suggest 'vs2' may instead be a generator-control feature not exposed in the observed 7-tab UI at all - idx53/54/57/58 (the current-based half of the vs2 group) could not be placed anywhere in the 102-field spec and are left unmapped.

Two separate bits exist (FlagsWord1 bit14 and FlagsWord0 bit15) for what the UI shows as a single 'Overruled by remote' checkbox; which bit corresponds to the visible field is not resolved. Both are recorded at certainty 'probable' in 'flags'.

Several fields decode in Amps (e.g. EPROM_vsonIInvHigh, EPROM_IInv2AES) while their ui-spec label states Watts. VEConfigure most likely multiplies by a fixed nominal voltage for display; the exact conversion was not verified against a live VEConfigure session.
